data.title: Beam Therapeutics shares jump on progress in precision genetic medicines and 2026 strategic plans
data.description:
• Beam Therapeutics (BEAM) shares surged 19% on Monday following  updates on the company’s continued efforts to create a sustainable, predictable model for advancing precision genetic medicines.
  • The company highlighted recent progress in its liver-targeted genetic disease and hematology franchises, outlined strategic priorities for 2026, and noted an extended anticipated operating runway supporting its plans.
  • Ended 2025 with an estimated $1.25 billion in cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities; projected operating runway extension into 2029 supports anticipated risto-cel launch and execution of BEAM-302 Pivotal Development Plan.
  • Furthermore, expansion of the liver-targeted genetic disease franchise is underway with a new program to be announced in the first half of 2026.

data.title: Wall Street moves lower amid Powell investigation
data.description:
Wall Street trades in the red on Monday after reports that the U.S. attorney’s office has opened a criminal investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell.

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was down by 0.1%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was down 0.1%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) fell 0.4%. 

From a sector vantage point, five of the S&P 500’s 11 sectors were in negative trading territory with Financials suffering the most.  At the other end of the spectrum, Consumer Staples have been the strongest performer on the session.

The investigation reportedly centers on the central bank’s renovation of its Washington headquarters and whether Powell lied to Congress about the project’s scope, according to the New York Times. 

Powell released an extraordinary statement, asserting that grand jury subpoenas served by the Department of Justice are not about legitimate legal concerns but rather punishment for the central bank’s refusal to set interest rates according to President Donald Trump’s preferences. 

U.S. Treasury yields were higher across the curve. The shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) moved up 1 basis point to 3.54%, while the U.S.10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) climbed 2 basis points to 4.19%. 

The economic calendar is light, with investors eyeing inflation data later this week in the form of the consumer price index and the producer price index. 

“We expect Tuesday’s CPI report to show inflation holding steady in the 2.7% range. While we may see a slight 'pop' in monthly goods prices due to ongoing tariff pass-throughs, the broader trend is clearly drifting toward our 2026 target of 2.7%–2.9%,” Robert Edwards, chief investment officer of Edwards Asset Management said. 

“2026 is likely to be a sawtooth year for the markets, where stocks experience a 7-15% pullback in the first half of the year for the simple reason that too much of Wall Street is bullish. The market is due for a sentiment reset and we expect that reset to take place in the next 6-months. This sentiment reset is necessary fuel for the next leg of the bull market. We expect stocks to reach record highs by year-end,” Edwards added.

data.title: Allurion expects FY25 net sales of 15M
data.description:
• Allurion Technologies (ALUR)  stated on Monday its preliminary net sales for Q4 of approximately $3.3M-$3.7M, representing a 22% to 37% increase.
  • The company expects FY25  net sales of approximately $15M.
  • The company reported improvement in operating expenses and operating loss both sequentially and YoY.
  • Consensus for FY25 and Q4 revenues are $14.55M and $2.95M.
  • Shares +1.79%.

data.title: Groupe Dynamite projects FY2025 comparable store sales growth guidance between 26.5% - 27%
data.description:
• Groupe Dynamite (GRGD:CA) on Monday announced comparable store sales growth of 30.8% for the first 9 weeks of the fourth quarter.
  • On that basis, Groupe Dynamite updated fiscal 2025 comparable store sales growth guidance to a range of 26.5% to 27.0%.
  • With respect to real estate activity, the company said it expects 20 gross store openings and 11 store closures, resulting in 9 net new store openings for fiscal 2025 and bringing total expected store count to 307 at year end.
  • Groupe Dynamite has also revised its previously communicated fiscal 2025 capital expenditure guidance to a range of $80 million to $90 million, from $85 million to $95 million, mainly reflecting payments timing.
  • GRGD:CA is +1.36% to $84.42.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Paramount files lawsuit against Warner Bros; intends to nominate director slate
data.description:
Paramount Skydance (PSKY) plans to nominate directors to Warner Bros. Discovery 's (WBD)  board as it continues its pursuit of the entertainment giant. Warner Bros. fell 1.1%.

Paramount Skydance (PSKY) also filed a lawsuit in Delaware court seeking to gain basic information to enable Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) shareholders to make an informed decision, according to a letter sent to Warner Bros. holders on Monday. 

Paramount (PSKY) plans to propose an amendment to Warner Bros.'s bylaws that will require WBD shareholder approval for any separation of Global Networks. 

"We do not undertake any of these actions lightly," the letter said. "Make no mistake, our goal remains to have constructive discussions with WBD's Board to reach an agreement that is in the best interests of WBD shareholders." 

Under the terms of the agreement announced December 5, Netflix (NFLX) will acquire Warner Bros. (WBD), including its film and television studios, HBO Max, and HBO, in a cash-and-stock transaction valued at $27.75 per WBD share, with a total enterprise value of approximately $82.7 billion (equity value of $72.0 billion).

data.title: Planet Labs secures 9-figure satellite deal from Sweden
data.description:
• Planet Labs (PL) shares jumped over 7% on Monday after the firm secured a multi-year low 9-figure agreement with the Swedish Armed Forces to deliver a suite of satellites, space-based data, and awareness solutions.
  • In the last year, Planet Labs has signed over half a billion dollars across three satellite services contracts, supporting the national security needs of Japan via JSAT and in collaboration with Germany.
  • Through the multi-year contract, Sweden will own a suite of Planet’s satellites, and have access to its high-resolution data, and intelligence solutions.

data.title: CVRx reports preliminary 2025 results, issues 2026 guidance
data.description:
• CVRx (CVRX) on Monday said that preliminary fourth-quarter 2025 revenue is expected to be $15.9 million to $16.1 million, up 4% to 5% from the same period in 2024.
  • Full year revenue expected to be $56.5 million to $56.7 million, representing growth of 10% to 11%.
  • The company reported 252 active implanting centers as of December 31, 2025, up from 250 at the end of September, and 53 U.S. sales territories, up three from the prior quarter.
  • Cash and cash equivalents were $75.7 million.
  • For fiscal 2026, CVRx expects total revenue of $63 million to $67 million, gross margin of 84% to 86%, and operating expenses of $103 million to $107 million.
  • First-quarter 2026 revenue is expected to be $13.7 million to $14.7 million.
  • Concensus for 2025 EPS is $-2.03 and revenue of $56.13M.
  • The company also said that the FDA has approved the BENEFIT-HF trial design in November 2025 and it has applied to CMS for coverage. 
  • CVRx also amended its debt facility to extend the maturity to 2031 and increase available funds to $100 million, drawing $10 million at closing.
  • CVRX -0.86% premarket to $8.04.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Stock market opens in red amid Powell investigation
data.description:
Wall Street fell on Monday amid news that the U.S. attorney’s office has launched a criminal investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell.

S&P 500 (SP500) -0.4%, the Dow (DJI) -0.6%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) -0.4%.

The investigation reportedly centers on the central bank’s renovation of its Washington headquarters and whether Powell lied to Congress about the project’s scope, according to the New York Times.

Powell released an extraordinary statement, asserting that grand jury subpoenas served by the Department of Justice are not about legitimate legal concerns but rather punishment for the central bank’s refusal to set interest rates according to President Donald Trump’s preferences.

Treasury yields were mixed across the curve. The U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) rose slightly to 3.56%. The benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose to 4.20%, adding 2 basis points. The 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) climbed to 4.85%, up 3 basis points.

data.title: Veracyte reports Q4 preliminary revenue in the range of $138M - $140M
data.description:
• Veracyte (VCYT) said on Monday that it expects to report Q4 total revenue between $138M and $140M, an increase of between 16% and 18%.
  • It sees testing revenue between $134M and $136M and testing volume of ~45,500.
  • For the full year ended Veracyte expects to report total revenue between $515M and $517M.
  • Testing revenue of between $491m and $493M for the year and testing volume of ~169,700, an increase of 19%.
  • Additionally, Veracyte expects to report adjusted EBITDA margin of greater than 25% for the full year ended December 31, 2025.
  • Veracyte initiated 2026 total revenue guidance of 10% to 13% growth, or $570 million to $582 million, with testing revenue guidance of 14% to 16% growth, or $559 million to $569 million, excluding the contribution from new tests. Further, 2026 adjusted EBITDA margin is expected to be 25%.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Medtronic gains FDA clearance for MiniMed Go insulin dosing system app
data.description:
• The US FDA has granted 510(k) clearance to Medtronic (MDT) for its MiniMed Go app that is used with the MiniMed Go Smart Multiple Daily Injection (MDI) system.
  • MiniMed Go integrates the InPen smart insulin pen and the Instinct sensor manufactured by Abbott (ABT) through the MiniMed Go app providing real-time glucose data, dose calculations, missed dose alerts, and actionable guidance.
